title: Describe the 4 major layers of an LLM system and how each fails in production.
prompt:
Describe the 4 major layers of an LLM system and how each fails in production.

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
The four layers — Model, Prompt, Retrieval, and Orchestration — fail in distinct ways with different operational signals. Model layer: unannounced provider updates, latency spikes under load, rate limits and request queuing, sudden safety-policy refusals. Prompt layer: degradation on real user queries, edge cases ignoring the rules, adversarial prompt injections, ambiguous formatting rules. Retrieval layer: semantically similar but irrelevant chunks, phrasing mismatches that miss the right info, stale or contradictory docs, bad chunk-splitting strategy. Orchestration layer: infinite agent loops, unisolated session context mixing between users, missing retry logic on API errors, hanging requests without timeouts.

**Takeaway:** Each layer needs its own monitoring signal — a symptom at the surface (a bad answer) could originate from any of the four layers, and you need layer-specific instrumentation to tell them apart.
hint_notes: Model = provider/latency issues, Prompt = rule/injection issues, Retrieval = relevance/staleness issues, Orchestration = looping/state/timeout issues.
